summary: Iter2 full-suite testing of GitHub integration, .gauntletignore seeding, 100-point audit, file upload/download, BYO agents, plus frontend AUDIT tab + GitHub panel + mobile shell. Backend: 31/31 pytest passing (16 new iter2 + 15 iter1 regression). Frontend: /ide loads clean with valid session cookie, no console errors, no duplicate-tab warning (iter1 fix landed), AUDIT tab renders 71/B score with all 7 breakdown bars and 4 recommendations, GitHub panel shows CONNECT TOKEN button + opens PAT form, mobile dock (414x800) toggles FILES/TERM/J drawers and scrim closes drawer on outside tap.

minor.issue: Recommendations only carry 'target_file' for the 'five_masters' category. The Five Masters scoring counts only error-severity issues toward score reduction, so 'warning' issues like range(len) (iter1 finding) leave ast_pts at 40/40 and no five_masters rec is emitted — meaning the audit's code-level 'PROPOSE FIX' affordance is never triggered for warning-only smells. Frontend can still show non-targeted recs (docs/tests/hygiene) but those have no target_file and cannot be auto-refined.

Upload accepts any binary, but audit's file_count only counts CODE_EXTS (.py/.js/.ts/...). Uploading a .txt/.md will not increment file_count. Consider clarifying file_count semantics in API docs or counting all non-ignored files.

issues: Dock right-padding (pr-28) leaves a gap behind the docked 'Made with Emergent' watermark — intentional but visually crowds the J button. Confirm with design.

action_items: Decide whether 'warning'-severity Five Masters issues should reduce the score and emit a five_masters recommendation with target_file (so PROPOSE FIX becomes reachable for range(len)-style smells). Same root cause as iter1's korotkevich finding.
(Optional) Document or normalise file_count semantics (code files vs all files) since the upload feature accepts arbitrary binaries.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1237 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Mixes auth, projects, files, governance, terminal, git, AI, GitHub, audit, upload, agents. Strongly recommend splitting into routers: github_router, projects_router, agents_router, audit_router, ai_router.
audit_project() in scoring.py reads every file twice (once for Five Masters, once for destructive scan). For larger workspaces this doubles I/O; cache file text per pass.

/api/projects/{id}/upload uses path query param + multipart; if path is supplied with traversal segments safe_join must guard against ../ (verify in safe_join). Add an explicit max-upload-bytes check (no Content-Length cap visible).
scrim closes on outside tap; correctly wired. iter1 duplicate-README-tab key warning is no longer present — fix verified in this run.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (audit, github connect form, mobile drawers + scrim all green; no console errors)

seed_data_creation: Tests auto-create TEST_iter2 project and clean up TEST_* agents at start of each run. Re-uses user_test_devspace seeded in iter1.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression at /app/backend/tests/ now covers iter1 + iter2 (31 tests). All endpoints listed in the iter2 review pass status + payload assertions. GitHub clone of sovereign-shards intentionally NOT tested (no valid PAT, per agent note). Mobile shell verified at 414x800: dock toggles FILES/TERM/J, scrim closes drawer on outside-tap. Audit's target_file affordance only fires when Five Masters drops below 40/40 — note for any frontend test asserting PROPOSE FIX button visibility.
rca_of_the_issue: Audit-recommendation target_file gap: scoring.py only emits five_masters recs (with target_file) when ast_pts < 40. fivemasters.evaluate() only deducts score for error-severity findings, so warning-tier smells (e.g. for-i-in-range(len)) cap at ast_pts == 40 even with multiple files flagged. Fix: drop weighted score per warning (e.g., 0.5/issue) inside scoring.py, OR always emit target_file recs for the worst-scoring files when their per-file issue_count > 0 regardless of overall ast_pts.
